propositions: Provide a single asymmetric signing substrate reused by the authorship and operation-cert epics instead of duplicating security-critical crypto.
Implement a DSSE envelope signing PAE(payloadType, body) over exact opaque bytes so verification never re-serializes parsed JSON and never trusts an in-band algorithm.

Add an SSHSIG scheme via ssh-keygen -Y in a subprocess with no new core dependency, verifying offline and failing closed on tampered bytes, wrong namespace, unknown principal, substituted key, and expired window.
Use a pluggable scheme registry with a per-kind policy table pinning {scheme, namespace, trust-root} out-of-band so the verifier never selects the scheme from record data.
Register the existing HMAC signer as a legacy scheme so plan-review and completion-verifier attestations still verify without regression.
Detect ssh-keygen availability and version (>=8.9) and fail closed with a clear error when absent, plus record an ADR documenting the substrate.
